name: Balsamic Chicken
instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 425 degrees F (220 degrees C).

2. Slice the Chicken Breasts in half horizontally to create thinner, even pieces that will cook faster and more uniformly.

3. In a small bowl, mix together the Italian Seasoning, Garlic Powder, Onion Powder, Salt, and Black Pepper. Season the chicken pieces generously on all sides with this spice mixture.

4. Heat the Olive Oil in a large ovenproof skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering.

5. Add the seasoned chicken to the hot skillet and sear until golden brown, 3-4 minutes per side. The chicken doesn't need to be fully cooked at this point. Remove the chicken to a plate and set aside.

6. Add the Orzo Pasta to the same skillet along with the Chicken Broth and Balsamic Vinegar, stirring to scrape up any browned bits from the bottom. Return the chicken to the skillet along with any accumulated juices.

7. Scatter the Cherry Tomatoes around the chicken in the skillet. Top each chicken piece with slices of Mozzarella cheese.

8. Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake until the chicken reaches an internal temperature of 165 degrees F (74 degrees C) and the orzo is tender, about 20 minutes.

9. If desired, broil for 2 minutes to achieve a golden, bubbly cheese topping.

10. Remove from the oven, sprinkle with torn Fresh Basil leaves, and drizzle with Balsamic Glaze before serving.
simpleInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425 degrees F (220 degrees C).

2. Slice Chicken Breasts horizontally and season with mixed Italian Seasoning, Garlic Powder, Onion Powder, Salt, and Black Pepper.

3. Sear seasoned chicken in hot Olive Oil in an ovenproof skillet until golden, then remove.

4. Add Orzo Pasta, Chicken Broth, and Balsamic Vinegar to skillet. Return chicken with juices.

5. Add Cherry Tomatoes and top chicken with Mozzarella slices.

6. Bake until chicken is cooked through and orzo is tender, about 20 minutes. Broil briefly if needed for golden cheese.

7. Garnish with Fresh Basil and drizzle with Balsamic Glaze.
